chunking.reason: large model/buffer allocations are at or above the 512MiB bridge clamp; future failures should distinguish splitting/chunk transport from shader lowering

diagnostic_bisection.method: binary-search fault isolation over API, graph, ICD, executor, and readback boundaries

nodes.question: Does the same model and server API produce deterministic CPU/no-offload answers?
Does GPU/offload output match CPU/no-offload at the HTTP completion boundary?
Do CPU and GPU agree on selected/top token probabilities before sampling policy can hide the error?
Did generic SPIR-V reach the Android Vulkan executor and complete successfully?
Does the largest/final-projection-like writable binding contain finite float samples after dispatch?
Do non-aliased read-only descriptor bindings match immediately after upload, then remain stable through dispatch?
Did requested bridge tuning environment variables reach the executor as dispatch evidence?
